title: Prevent sensitive issue details in supply-chain error logs
outOfScope: Changing secret detection patterns or scan semantics
Adding secret values to diagnostics or test fixtures
Changing release workflow or public release assets

sources.reason: CodeQL identified direct logging of issue details in the supply-chain checker.
Existing supply-chain tests provide the regression boundary for safe failure output.
Repository workflow defines the Contract and Summary evidence required for this code change.

riskAssessment.reason: Failure diagnostics must remain actionable without echoing issue content that a future scanner may classify as sensitive.

executionDecision.reason: The CodeQL finding is bounded to the error-output boundary and can be fixed with a tested non-disclosure behavior.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm failure output does not include issue details while preserving a non-zero failure result.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A failed supply-chain check returns status 1 and emits only a fixed summary with the issue count, not issue contents.
A regression test proves issue details are absent from stderr for a failing check.
Existing supply-chain checks and repository quality gates remain passing.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: CodeQL reports that the supply-chain checker writes issue values directly to logs, creating a future clear-text disclosure path if a scanner includes sensitive content in an issue string.
constraints: Do not change scan semantics or return codes.
Do not print secret values, issue details, or derived sensitive content.
Keep diagnostics deterministic and useful enough to identify the failing command.
intent.rationale: A fixed failure summary removes the clear-text logging sink while preserving fail-closed command behavior.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Failing supply-chain check does not disclose issue details
Successful supply-chain check behavior remains unchanged

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
